description: I try use it everywhere if it's suitable.
author: Rykmas M.
pros: I was leading several projects which were using regular MySQL replication. This lead us to some issues from time to time. It all changed when we migrated to Percona XtraDB Cluster. It was full suitable for our cases.
cons: Automatic provisioning, you don't need to care about lot of aspects.

review_title: Perfect value to price and flexibility
author: Yoann L.
pros: Ease of administration, ease of automation, verbose log informations A massive load of studies and ressources are available for nearly every subjects With information_schema, performance_schema, innodb_metrics and status, you can basically measure performance of every single pieces of internal structures and tune accordingly
cons: HA components (galera) log a lot of useful infos, but some errors remains extremely hard to exploit. EG if nodes lost communication, regain it and proceed to auto-bootstrap themselves we only have a bunch of dumped internal c++ objects with obfuscated field names, without any filename to read source. Plus Github does not support "sub" repository code queries, Galera components fall under this category which makes it harder to really grasp what is going on

review_title: PXC that supports business applications for stakeholders
author: Udbhav S.
pros: Has advanced data security and strong encryption, having a facility to store a large amount of data
cons: Sometimes take a longer time to load a bulk data

review_title: PXC Has been Great For US
description: Overall, we are very happy that we have moved to PXC.
author: Christopher G.
pros: We switched over from MariaDB to PXC 5.7 and we have seen improvements with our database performance. PXC has been able to handle a large transaction amounts with better ease. And the system has been more stable since the switch as well.
cons: The only negatives to our switch to PXC 5.7 have been that we did need to update some stored procedures in order for it to work the same as they were in MariaDB and we had to adjust the strict mode settings for now to allow our code to fully work.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: Not reliable in 2013. Test the product thoroughly for all your scenarios with realistic workloads.
author: Harjit L.
pros: Having multi nodes across two site was a good idea for DR purposes.
cons: I used an older version in 2013 the whole cluster would hang if all nodes on the DR site did not keep up - which they didn't as it was WAN.

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: It has High availability and MySQL clustering solution that secures the system. It has Open source feature and Cost efficient solution. It has powerful ability in configuration process . It is simple enough to grasp administrative tasks.
cons: The complexities are the main problem that occurs.

description: PXC is better than master slave topology. Application teams are happy using this product
author: Pradeep R.
pros: I like the customer support of Percona, whenever there is P1 they jump into the call and troubleshoot the isuue
cons: For large databases, SST is the problem it takes longer time. May this can be avoided in future releases

description: If you want to use a better product than MySQL, you can definitely choose xtradb from MySQL developers. It has built-in clustering, already tuned performance and better documentation.
author: Cihan T.
pros: It has many features than MySQL. It mixed with clustering and security. Strongly recommended for production usage. Community help is acceptable and their documentation is too good.
cons: At documentation, it can be much easier if they explain more detailed. Such topics at cluster, you need to find a solution by googling.

description: I am liking the Percona XtraDB Cluster.
author: KARTHICK KUMAR N.
pros: Easy to install and manage the products.
cons: Nothing major, easily we understand the basic concepts and functional.

author: Kumar S.
pros: I like user friendly interface and must easy in use.
cons: I faced some lag in software in heavy data using
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: One of the best solution in the market for medium sized clusters
author: Donatas A.
pros: The best part is multi-master replication. We always used master-master replication with ExaZK [SENSITIVE CONTENT HIDDEN] in front to have a smooth failover. With XtraDB it's kinda easier because you don't need any external services for that. That's why this product is best suited for medium sized setups.
cons: We used XtraDB in geo-replication setup, but it was slow due to high latencies and all nodes have to ACK writes before returning a response. We described some details in our blog post: [SENSITIVE CONTENT HIDDEN]

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The most significant feature is to efficiently manage the recruitment processes through databases and opportunities of jobs. Also the interactive feature to compare the job profiles.
cons: curretly i dont find any inefficiencies.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Sejal C.
pros: Easy and attractive to use dashboard security
cons: The user manual can be more friendly and informative for beginners
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Gargi G.
pros: Features of this product are good in nature and helpful
cons: The interface can be made better otherwise its helpful product
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

author: Abhishek V.
pros: Everything, easy to use, easy to implement, flexible, user friendly, nice interface.
